38.reason: The increment of i here skips the first non-zero digit that was already printed, potentially causing incorrect output and missing digits.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Incrementing `i` before checking may skip a valid digit or access out of bounds, and combined with loop bounds causes a trailing decimal point when no fractional digits exist.

10.reason: loop condition should be s[i]!='\0' to process all characters; using s[i+1] misses the last character if no newline is present after the number.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  loop condition should be s[i]!='\0' to process all characters; using s[i+1] misses the last character if no newline is present after the number.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Loop condition is unconventional and after loop the index i ends one past the last processed character, leading to incorrect null-termination later. Also, the loop does not properly handle the newline from fgets.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  The loop condition s[i+1]!='\0' stops before processing the last character, which is then overwritten by s[i]='\0';, causing data loss if the input does not end with a newline. It should be s[i]!='\0'.

20.reason: should be s[j]='\0' to correctly terminate the string at the position of the last copied character; otherwise duplicate digits remain from the original array.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Should set s[j]='\0' instead of s[i]. This leaves duplicate characters in the string (characters from j to i-1 remain), causing incorrect output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  This overwrites the last character of the string. After fixing the loop condition, the string should be properly terminated at s[j]='\0'; instead.

23.reason: The condition `i<strlen(s)-1` causes an off-by-one error, skipping the last character of the string and preventing correct processing of all digits.
